title: Snake

formal_elements_framework.player: One player controls the snake; the system generates food and constraints.
formal_elements_framework.procedures: Use directional input to control movement
rules: Eating food increases length and score
Collision with walls or self ends the game
Cannot reverse direction instantly (in most versions)

dilemmas: Risk vs Reward: Move into tight spaces to reach food vs stay safe in open space
Short-term vs Long-term: Eat immediately vs position for future survival
Path Planning: Direct path vs safer longer route
Growth vs Control: Longer snake increases score but reduces maneuverability
Space Management: Use space aggressively vs preserve it for late game

formal_elements_framework.outcome: Game ends on collision; result is final score

core_loop: Move continuously, Navigate toward food, Eat and grow, Avoid collisions, Repeat until failure, Fail, Restart
onboarding: Minimal. Players learn through immediate interaction and failure.

Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi, Flow: The Psych.challenge: Increasing difficulty due to growth and limited space
Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi, Flow: The Psych.Negative feedback / Flow Equilibrium: Failure resets progress and encourages retry
Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi, Flow: The Psych.Autotelic Experience (Intrinsic Motivati: Movement and optimization are intrinsically satisfying

dramatic_elements.premise: An abstract snake consumes food and grows within limited space
dramatic_elements.dramatic_arc: Low tension (early game), Rising tension (mid game), High tension (late game), Crash (resolution)
